narrative.headline: 2020 Kia Sorento: the engine dominates owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 128 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is by far the most reported area at 49% (63 reports), tied to 4 fires, 1 crash, and 2 injuries. The electrical system is next at 14% and stands out for fires (6). The power train (13%) and reports about the fuel/propulsion system and trailer hitches (7% each) round out the list. No deaths are reported, but multiple areas involve fire, so check the engine bay and wiring carefully.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: By far the biggest complaint: 63 reports (49%), with 4 fires, 1 crash, and 2 injuries. Watch for overheating and warning chimes while driving.

issues.sample: The contact owned a 2020 Kia Sorento. The contact stated that while the vehicle was parked, her daughter saw smoke coming out of the vehicle. The contact stated that as she opened the vehicle, she saw flames on the front side of the vehicle. The contact was able to extinguish the
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 18 reports (14%), including 6 with fire. The high fire count makes this worth a close look.

issues.sample: I HAVE A 2020 KIA SORENTO V6. WHEN THE ENGINE IS COLD OR HAS BEEN SITTING FOR AN HOUR OR SO, THE TRANSMISSION SHIFTS ERRATICALLY BETWEEN 20 AND 40 MPH. WHEN COMING TO A STOP, AT THE SAME SPEED, THE VEHICLE SURGES FORWARD. I HAVE TAKEN THE VEHICLE BACK TO THE DEALERSHIP 3 TIMES. T
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 17 reports (13%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries noted. Complaints center on how the vehicle shifts and accelerates.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 9 reports (7%), including 1 crash and 1 fire. A smaller but recurring area.

issues.title: Trailer Hitches
issues.blurb: 9 reports (7%), with 1 fire. Several reference NHTSA recall campaign 22V703000.
